results.modality_check: Equal-or-stronger restrictive prohibition ('shall not') satisfies the regulatory prohibition
results.other_constraints_check: Timing is compatible because approval must exist before engagement; however the contract omits the regulation's explicit 'specific or general written' authorization detail
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall not engage a Sub-processor, unless this is approved by the Controller by

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient match: processor must inform controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors
results.condition_check: same condition: in case of general written authorization/authorisation
results.modality_check: shall on contract side matches regulatory shall
results.other_constraints_check: no conflicting manner, timing, target, location, or cause; contract is at least as specific and adds controller objection mechanics without weakening the notice duty
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require the processor to inform the controller of intended changes involving addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply in the case of general written authorization/authorisation.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall', matching the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract includes the same subject matter and adds an opportunity for objection and a 30-day deemed acceptance period, which do not weaken the informing duty.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor remains liable to controller for performance of sub-processor obligations, matching the regulation's full liability duty
results.condition_check: No condition in either text
results.modality_check: Contract uses 'shall', equal force to regulatory 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: No differing manner, timing, target, location, or cause; contract is substantively equivalent
results.contract_source_text: Processor shall remain liable to the Controller for the performance of its Sub- processor's obligations.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object match: processor must ensure authorized persons processing personal data are bound by confidentiality or statutory confidentiality obligation
results.condition_check: No condition in either side
results.modality_check: Contract uses an affirmative mandatory statement equivalent to shall
results.other_constraints_check: No differing manner, timing, target, location, or cause
results.contract_source_text: Processor ensures that persons authorised to process the Personal Data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality.

results.core_alignment_check: The contract expressly requires the processor to inform the controller of the legal requirement before processing, with the same public-interest prohibition carve-out. This matches the regulation's processor-to-controller notification duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered when processing is required by Union/Member State law, and both exclude cases where the law prohibits informing the controller on public-interest grounds.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: the contract uses 'shall' for the notification duty, matching the regulation's mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'before Processing' matches 'before processing'; recipient and object are the same substantive notification.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires a security measure ensuring restoration of availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident. The contract sub-item, read with the parent security clause, expressly includes 'the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident,' which is the same substantive obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered by a physical or technical incident.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The parent clause is mandatory ('shall implement') and the sub-item is part of that mandatory measures list; the contract is at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'In a timely manner' matches the regulation's timing requirement, and the object/target is the same personal data availability and access restoration measure.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The contract expressly requires the Controller and the Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk, which matches the processor's Art. 32(1) duty in substance and is at least as strong (indeed broader, since it includes the controller too).
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on taking into account the state of the art, implementation costs, and the nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, together with the risk of varying likelihood and severity to rights and freedoms.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall', which is mandatory and matches the regulation's obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The timing/location/target are not materially different, and the security standard ('appropriate to the risk') is the same substantive requirement; the listed examples are additional detail, not a weakening.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: The same actor (processor) is required to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures. The contract requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures as well, aimed at GDPR compliance and protection of data subjects' rights. This is substantively the same security/organizational-measures obligation and is at least as protective.
results.condition_check: No triggering condition in either clause; compatible.
results.modality_check: The contract's 'agrees to' is an undertaking to implement the measures and is not weaker in substance than the regulatory 'shall' in this DPA context.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is not looser on manner or scope; it requires measures that ensure GDPR compliance and protection of rights, which is compatible with and at least as strict as ensuring security appropriate to risk.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: The processor obligation matches: it allows for and contributes to audits/inspections. The contract covers audits including inspections conducted by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller, which is substantively within the regulation's scope of audits conducted by the controller or another auditor mandated by the controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds reasonable qualifiers (during normal business hours, reasonably necessary, confidentiality-bound auditor, purpose tied to Article 28 obligations) but does not narrow away the audit/inspection duty in a materially incompatible way.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract imposes a mandatory obligation ('to allow for and contribute'), which is at least as strong as 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'During normal business hours' and 'reasonably necessary' are reasonable, expected constraints and do not make the duty looser than the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: to allow for and contribute during normal business hours to reasonably necessary audits including inspections, conducted by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller, solely for the purpose of fulfilment of the Controller's obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR and for accurately stipulated research questions in this connection provided that such external qualified auditor is subject to and bound by confidentiality obligations as stipulated in clause 12 of this Agreement;

results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses a mandatory plain statement within the parent clause context, which is at least as strong as the regulatory requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the subject matter, target, and scope align; the contract addresses the same security-measures effectiveness testing/evaluation obligation and does not add a looser timing or narrower scope.

In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: both require the processor to ensure that persons under its authority with access to personal data process that data only on the controller's instructions; same substantive obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract adds an exception where processing is required under applicable EU law or national legislation, which does not weaken the core instruction-only rule.
results.modality_check: Equal-or-greater force: 'must ensure' matches 'shall ensure'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'persons performing work for the Processor' is substantively equivalent to 'any natural person acting under its authority'; 'Personal Data' and 'such Personal Data' match the same object.

results.core_alignment_check: processor obligation to ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions vs processor itself shall process personal data only on documented instructions from controller; same substantive instruction-only duty, but contract does not expressly cover 'any natural person acting under its authority' as such
results.condition_check: No condition in regulation; contract imposes unconditional instruction-only processing, which is not weaker
results.modality_check: Shall / only on documented instructions is mandatory and at least as strong as required
results.other_constraints_check: Timing/manner compatible; documented instructions are stricter than instructions, and no conflicting location/target/cause issues
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract triggers assistance 'where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing,' which is not weaker than the regulation's high-risk / lack-of-mitigation trigger and is a reasonable equivalent qualifier for when assistance is needed.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'to assist' in a contractual obligation is mandatory in context and matches the regulation's 'shall assist'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required' matches 'consulting the supervisory authorities prior to processing'; the timing and target are substantively the same.

results.condition_check: Compatible: both are unconditional instruction-only processing, with the contract adding a lawful-processing exception and notice duty, which is not weaker

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation prohibits the processor from transferring personal data to a third country or international organization unless the controller has given prior specific or general authorization. shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor itself to return or delete all personal data to the controller after service ends, while the contract only requires the processor to assist the controller with deleting and/or returning data upon the controller's request; assistance is a different, narrower obligation and does not itself impose the return/delete duty.
results.condition_check: Not compatible as a direct match: the contract is triggered only if the controller requests assistance, whereas the regulation requires action after the end of services regardless of a request.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall' for the assistance duty, but that duty is not the same as the mandatory return/delete obligation in the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing is also narrower/different: 'during and upon the expiry of the Agreement' plus request-based assistance is not equivalent to the processor's obligation to act after the end of the provision of services relating to processing.
results.contract_source_text: If Controller requests the Processor’s assistance to delete and/or return (i.e. export) the Controller’s data including the Personal Data during and upon the expiry of the Agreement such assistance shall be rendered by Processor at Controller’s expense and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 4. 10. Sub-processing

results.core_alignment_check: The processor obligation to process personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions matches the regulation's requirement to process personal data on documented instructions from the controller; both address the same processing-authorisation duty. The added reference to clause 2 and the transfer carve-out do not change the core obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds an exception where Union or Member State law requires processing, which is consistent with GDPR-style lawful-compulsion carve-outs and does not weaken the instruction-based rule.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall' / 'only in accordance with' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'Only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' is substantively equivalent to 'on documented instructions from the controller'; the explicit inclusion of third-country/international-organisation transfers is within the same or stricter scope.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Content
results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap: the contract requires the notification to describe the nature of the personal data breach, which matches one required element, but it does not include the other required items (DPO name/contact details, consequences, mitigation measures).
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause; compatible.
results.modality_check: Shall on the contract side matches the mandatory regulation, but only for the narrower content stated.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause constraints at issue.
results.contract_source_text: The aforementioned notification shall describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach including where possible:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Content
results.core_alignment_check: Same general subject (personal data breach notification) but the contract only requires describing the nature of the breach, while the regulation requires the notification to include additional mandatory items: DPO contact details, consequences, and mitigation measures. The contract does not cover those substantive elements.
results.condition_check: No condition in the regulation; contract adds 'where possible' only to the nature description, which is not a weakening issue for that item.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), but the contract obligation is narrower in scope.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location issues. The contract is substantively incomplete because it omits required content items.
results.contract_source_text: The aforementioned notification shall describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach including where possible:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Content
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item states the likely consequences of the Personal Data Breach; this is the same content obligation.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: The sub-item is within a mandatory parent clause ('shall describe'), so the plain statement counts as mandatory and is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints; 'likely consequences' is substantively aligned with 'consequences' and is not looser in this context.
results.contract_source_text: the likely consequences of the Personal Data Breach;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient match: processor must inform controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization
results.condition_check: same condition: in case of general written authorization
results.modality_check: contract is mandatory ('shall') and at least as strong as regulation
results.other_constraints_check: no conflicting timing/manner/location/target constraints; added objection mechanics do not weaken the notification duty
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: processor cooperation with the supervisory authority on the controller’s request is related to assistance, but the regulation specifically requires assisting the controller in consulting supervisory authorities prior to processing; the contract instead cooperates with the authority in performing its tasks and does not clearly cover the controller’s prior-consultation duty
results.condition_check: request by the controller is not the same as the high-risk / absence-of-mitigation trigger for prior consultation
results.modality_check: plain statement is mandatory enough, but modality does not cure the mismatch
results.other_constraints_check: timing is materially different: the regulation requires assistance prior to processing, while the contract has no prior-to-processing consultation obligation
results.contract_source_text: to cooperate (including representatives of Processor), on the Controller’s request, with the supervisory authority in the performance of its tasks;

results.core_alignment_check: Processor imposing the same obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract/legal act matches the regulation's requirement to impose the Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply when the processor engages sub-processors for processing activities.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' imposes a mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract uses the same legal mechanism ('by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law') and is at least as specific/strict on implementation guarantees.
results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: The contract requires the Processor to process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller, which matches the regulation's requirement that the processor process personal data on documented instructions from the controller. The parent clause also expressly states that the Agreement constitutes the instructions as of the date of the Agreement, reinforcing the documented-instructions element.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are unconditional duties tied to documented instructions from the controller.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall' and the 'only on' restriction, which is at least as strong as the regulation's mandatory requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'only on documented instructions from the Controller' matches 'on documented instructions from the controller'; the additional authorization language and means-of-processing language do not weaken this duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/manner align: processor processes personal data only in accordance with controller instructions, which matches documented instructions from the controller
results.condition_check: Compatible; contract adds an exception for legal requirements and notice to controller, which does not weaken the core instruction-based processing duty
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger; 'shall' with 'only in accordance with' is mandatory and restrictive, not permissive
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible; 'only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' is substantively equivalent to 'on documented instructions from the controller', and the added transfer reference is within scope/stricter
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, while the contract only requires compliance with GDPR and applicable law; it does not mention documented controller instructions or restrict processing to them
results.condition_check: No matching condition; the contract lacks the 'on documented instructions from the controller' trigger
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory, but it imposes a different obligation rather than the required instruction-based processing duty
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive overlap on manner; the contract addresses legal compliance in general, not the specific documented-instructions manner
results.contract_source_text: To ensure that the Personal Data collected by Controller is done in accordance with the relevant provisions of the GDPR and other applicable EU and national data protection law in the Member State in which the Controller is established (and where applicable has been notified to the relevant authorities of that Member State) and does not violate the relevant provisions of that Member State. 5. General obligations of the Processor The Processor agrees:

results.core_alignment_check: processor/persons under its authority with access to personal data: the contract requires the Processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the Controller, which is substantively the same instruction-only restriction for persons acting under the processor's authority
results.condition_check: Both apply generally to processing personal data by persons with access/processor processing; the contract is at least as strict because it requires documented instructions from the Controller.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall' restriction, which is equal-or-greater force than the regulation's 'shall ensure'.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting timing, manner, target, location, or cause; 'documented instructions' is compatible with and stricter than 'instructions'.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must make available compliance-demonstrating information to controller; contract requires processor to make records available to controller and supervisory authority on request, which is substantively the same information-access/audit-support obligation and can satisfy the GDPR duty via records
results.condition_check: on request is compatible with the regulation's make-available duty; the regulation does not require unconditional proactive delivery and request-based availability is at least as protective in this context
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: records instead of information is acceptable under the same-information principle; availability to the supervisory authority is additional/stricter, and no conflicting timing/manner/location constraints appear
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall make the records available to the Controller and the supervisory authority on request. 8. Costs for assistance and audits

results.core_alignment_check: The processor obligation is to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure GDPR-compliant processing and protection/security of personal data; this matches the regulation's requirement that the processor take all measures required under Article 32 or otherwise ensure the security of processing.
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause; compatible.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'agrees to' but imposes a substantive obligation to implement measures, which is at least as strong as 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's manner clause is equivalent and broader: measures must make processing meet GDPR requirements and protect data subjects' rights, which includes security of processing and Article 32-type measures.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match the same obligation: the contract assists the Controller with DPIA and prior consultation of supervisory authorities, while the regulation requires assistance in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
results.condition_check: compatible in general assistance-trigger terms, but the underlying task is different
results.modality_check: obligation force is sufficient for the assistance duty, but it is directed to a different duty
results.other_constraints_check: no material mismatch on timing/manner beyond the different substantive task
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: processor obligation to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security of processing aligns with processor duty to take all measures required under Article 32 / ensure security of processing
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are risk-based security measures taking into account state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes, and risk to rights and freedoms
results.modality_check: Compatible: contract uses 'shall' and is at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: contract requires appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, which matches or exceeds the regulation's security obligation
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require technical and organisational measures to ensure security of processing, specifically confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience of processing systems and services; the contract sub-item is part of the parent mandatory security-measures clause.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on taking account of the state of the art, implementation costs, processing context/purpose, and risk to rights and freedoms; the contract is substantively the same and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall implement' and thus imposes an equal-or-greater mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract requires measures 'to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk' and includes the same security attributes; this is substantively aligned with the regulation's security requirement.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align only on assistance, but the object and cause are different: regulation requires assisting the controller in ensuring security of processing, while the contract requires assisting the controller with responding to data subject rights requests

results.core_alignment_check: Same actor and general liability subject matter, but the contract states a general liability rule under Danish law and clause 13 limitations, not the specific GDPR processor-liability trigger limited to non-compliance with processor-directed GDPR obligations or acting outside/contrary to controller instructions.
results.condition_check: The regulation has a specific limiting condition; the contract does not include that condition and instead uses a different, broader legal framework and clause-based limitation.

results.other_constraints_check: The contract is materially looser/different on the scope and cause of liability; it does not track the regulation's restricted circumstances.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall be liable for damages in accordance with the general rules of Danish law subject to the limitations set out in this clause 13.

results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to make available to the controller information necessary to demonstrate the processor's own compliance with Article 28 obligations, while the contract only addresses documenting a sub-processor's compliance and allows referral to audit reports from sub-processors.
results.condition_check: Compatible in that both are triggered by a controller request, but the contract's trigger is specifically a request to document sub-processor compliance, not the processor's Article 28 compliance.
results.modality_check: The contract is weaker/more permissive: it says the processor is entitled to fulfil the obligation by referring the controller to reports, rather than imposing a direct duty to make available the required information.

results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. results.core_alignment_check: Processor must inform Controller of intended sub-processor changes and give opportunity to object; regulation requires Controller right to object after being informed. Same real-world obligation, with the contract also expressly granting the opportunity to object.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply in the case of general written authorization/authorisation and concern intended changes to addition or replacement of sub-processors.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: the contract states the Controller is given the opportunity to object and further says changes are deemed accepted only if no objection within 30 days; this preserves and operationalizes the right to object, while the prohibition on objection in one circumstance is a separate limitation not shown to undercut the general right required here.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract requires notification before the objection window runs, matching the requirement that the controller object after being informed by the processor.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to make available to the controller information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations, while the contract only lets the processor satisfy a request to document a sub-processor’s compliance by referring the controller to audit reports/similar documentation from sub-processors; this is narrower and about sub-processor compliance documentation, not the processor’s own Article 28 compliance information.
results.condition_check: Compatible in part: both are triggered in a controller-request context, but the contract is specifically limited to a request to document sub-processor compliance, which is a different and narrower situation than the regulation’s general duty to make compliance information available.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall be entitled' to choose a method of fulfilment, which is permissive/optional as to the means and does not impose the same direct obligation to make information available.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract’s object and target are materially different: it concerns referring the controller to sub-processor audit reports and similar documentation, not providing the processor’s information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations; the audit-standard condition is additional but not the main issue.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. results.core_alignment_check: Processor must inform Controller of intended sub-processor addition/replacement changes and give opportunity to object; regulation requires Controller have right to object after being informed. results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply in case of general written authorization/authorisation.
results.modality_check: Compatible and stronger: contract imposes mandatory notification and expressly gives opportunity to object; it also restricts objection only in a narrower circumstance, but does not remove the basic right to object in the general case.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern the processor's ability to engage/add/replace sub-processors and the controller's prior written authorization/consent. The contract addresses the same real-world obligation via a general written authorization mechanism with notice and objection.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation requires prior specific or general written authorization; the contract covers the general written authorization case and provides a prior notice/opportunity to object, which is a recognized form of general authorization.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation prohibits engagement without prior authorization; the contract imposes notice and a deemed-acceptance mechanism under general authorization, which is not weaker than requiring authorization before changes take effect.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: timing is prior notice with a 30-day objection window, which is consistent with prior authorization. The written form and specificity/general nature are addressed through the general written authorisation and notification process.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires prior specific or general written authorization from the controller before engaging a sub-processor. This is the same underlying authorization mechanism for sub-processor engagement, with a prior notice-and-objection process under general authorization.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the context of general written authorization; the contract also covers intended changes to addition/replacement of sub-processors before they take effect.
results.modality_check: Compatible and not weaker: the regulation prohibits engagement without prior authorization; the contract requires notice and provides an objection window, which is at least as protective and functions as a prior authorization mechanism.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the regulation requires prior written authorization, and the contract's 30-day objection period operates before the changes are deemed accepted, satisfying the prior/written aspect.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes them only on the controller's instructions, while the contract only requires the processor to implement agreed changes to instructions and ensure those changes are implemented; it does not address restricting natural persons' processing to controller instructions.

results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk. The contract, read with the parent clause, requires the Controller and the Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, which is the same substantive security obligation and is at least as strict.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on taking into account the state of the art/costs/nature-scope-context-purposes and the risk of varying likelihood and severity to rights and freedoms.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall implement,' which is mandatory and matches the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's manner ('to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk') matches the regulation, and the listed examples are additional specifics that do not weaken the obligation.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires security measures that include restoring availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident. Same obligation, same substance.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered by a physical or technical incident; the contract matches the regulatory situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The parent clause imposes a mandatory obligation to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures, and the sub-item is part of that mandatory list.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'In a timely manner' matches the regulation's timing requirement and is not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to immediately inform the controller when an instruction infringes GDPR/data protection law. The contract instead says the processor will use reasonable endeavours to comply with legislative changes and addresses termination if the parties cannot agree on changes to instructions. It does not impose a duty to inform the controller of unlawful instructions.
results.condition_check: The triggering condition is different: the regulation is triggered by an instruction that infringes data protection law; the contract is triggered by legislative changes and failure to agree on implementation terms.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a weaker/indirect obligation ('reasonable endeavours') and no express duty to inform.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires immediate notice to the controller; the contract contains no comparable timing or notification duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires technical and organisational measures to ensure security, including encryption of personal data and ensuring confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services. The contract parent clause imposes the same general security-measures duty on the Controller and Processor, and the sub-item specifically includes encryption of personal data in transit and the ability to ensure confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services. This is the same obligation, not merely adjacent.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on risk/state of the art/nature, scope, context and purposes of processing and the rights and freedoms risk. The contract is substantively equivalent and appropriately qualified.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation's 'can include' lists examples of measures within the security obligation; the contract uses 'shall implement' and thus is at least as strong, with the listed measures included as part of the mandatory security measures.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's 'appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk' matches the regulation's security standard. The specific encryption measure is narrower in scope (transmitted via public networks and remote access) but still satisfies the example requirement as an included measure; the confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience item aligns directly.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for sub-processor performance, while the contract only requires notice of intended sub-processor changes and gives the controller an objection mechanism/acceptance by silence. That is a different obligation and does not address liability.
results.condition_check: The regulation has no triggering condition; the contract's general written authorisation / non-objection conditions are unrelated to liability.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory liability duty. The contract imposes mandatory notice/objection procedures, but not liability, so modality does not align on the required obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant match on timing, manner, target, location, or cause for the liability duty.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: the regulation requires the processor to immediately inform the controller about unlawful instructions, while the contract only authorizes processing and limits processing to documented instructions; it does not impose any duty to notify the controller of unlawful instructions.

results.modality_check: The contract contains mandatory processing-limit language, but no obligation to inform/notify, so modality for the required duty is absent.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent immediacy or notification timing is provided; the clause addresses authorization and processing scope, not reporting.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections conducted by the controller or the controller’s mandated auditor. results.other_constraints_check: Timing and manner differ materially: annual website publication of a report is not equivalent to allowing and contributing to audits/inspections by the controller or its auditor. The contract omits the inspection/audit participation aspect.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor, when assessing security, to take into account specific security risks to personal data. The contract instead requires the processor to impose equivalent data protection obligations on sub-processors and ensure appropriate technical and organisational measures. results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation prohibits the processor from transferring personal data to a third country or international organization unless the controller has given prior specific or general authorization. results.other_constraints_check: No substantive match on recipient/location target: the regulation concerns transfer to a third country or international organization; the contract concerns transfer to a third-party provider in connection with integration services.
results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires implementing appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security, including regular testing/assessment/evaluation of those measures. The contract parent clause imposes the same security-measures obligation on the Controller and Processor, but the cited sub-item only lists one measure (ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience) and does not itself include regular testing, assessing, or evaluating effectiveness.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are risk-based security measures taking into account context and risk; no conflict.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall' and is mandatory, which meets the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Not satisfied: the regulation specifically includes regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures, while the contract text provided does not state any regular testing/assessment/evaluation duty.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to take all measures required under Article 32 / ensure security of processing. The contract parent clause requires the Controller and the Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, which is substantively the same security obligation and at least as strict. The sub-item on encryption is an example measure within that broader duty, not a separate narrower-only duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on risk/state of the art/nature, scope, context and purposes of processing and the severity/likelihood of risks.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall implement', which is mandatory and not weaker than the regulation's 'shall take'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's 'appropriate technical and organisational measures' and 'to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk' match the regulation's security-of-processing requirement; the listed encryption measure is consistent and not contradictory.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to return or delete all personal data to the controller after processing ends. The contract context and sub-item address deletion of the controller’s data after a grace period / after ninety days if the agreement is not continued. This is related to post-termination deletion, but it does not clearly cover return of data, and the timing is tied to a grace period / non-renewal rather than expressly to the end of the provision of processing services. Still, the deletion obligation itself is substantively the same part of the regulation.

results.other_constraints_check: Timing is compatible enough because deletion occurs after ninety days / after expiry of the grace period, which is later but still post-termination. However, the contract does not address the alternative obligation to return data, only deletion.

results.modality_check: No relevant modality match; contract uses 'will' for different duties, not an obligation to assist security.
results.other_constraints_check: Different subject matter: deletion/return of data and access to functionality, not security of processing.
results.contract_source_text: Processor will give Controller access to system functionality in order for the Controller to delete and/or return (i.e. export) any and all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data during the Agreement. Upon expiry of the Agreement the Processor will delete all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data, unless EU or national Member State law requires storage of the Personal Data.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires processor measures to ensure processing security under Article 32; the contract parent clause requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security, including the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience, and the sub-item adds restoring availability and access to personal data after a physical or technical incident.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by security/incident-related processing risks; the contract is at least as specific and fits the Article 32 security context.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation is mandatory ('shall'); the parent clause is mandatory ('shall implement'), and the sub-item is part of that mandatory set.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'in a timely manner' is not weaker than the security/availability requirement and is consistent with restoring availability/access after an incident.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation gives the controller a right to object to changes involving addition/replacement of sub-processors after notice; the contract instead prohibits the processor from engaging a sub-processor unless the controller approves. This is the same substantive control over sub-processor changes, framed more strictly.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation applies in case of general written authorization and after notice; the contract requires controller approval before engagement, which is at least as protective and fits the same situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. A prohibition on engaging unless approved is stricter than a controller right to object.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract’s approval-before-engagement timing is equivalent or stricter than being informed and then objecting.

results.modality_check: The contract is a descriptive pointer to an appendix, not a mandatory in-text statement of duration.
results.other_constraints_check: Mismatch in object and locus: deletion timeframes are not the same as the duration of processing, and the relevant content is deferred to Appendix 4 rather than stated in the clause itself.
results.contract_source_text: The process and timeframes for deletion of Controller’s data including the Personal Data is described in Appendix 4, which forms an integral part of the Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of a legal requirement before processing, subject to a public-interest-law prohibition. The contract instead authorizes the processor to transfer/process controller data for third-party integration services when the controller chooses to use them; it does not impose any duty to inform the controller about legal requirements.
results.condition_check: Not applicable in substance: the contract condition concerns optional use of third-party integrations, not a legal prohibition on informing the controller for public-interest reasons.
results.modality_check: The contract is an authorization/permission, not a mandatory duty to inform.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing and object are different: the regulation is about notice before processing of a legal requirement, while the contract is about data transfer and processing in connection with integrations.
results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: Same general situation (general written authorization and intended addition/replacement of sub-processors), but the contract mainly imposes processor notice and a deemed-acceptance mechanism; it does not preserve an unconditional controller right to object after notice.

results.modality_check: Weaker/contradictory: the regulation requires the controller to have the right to object, while the contract says the controller cannot object in a specified case and deems changes accepted if no objection within 30 days.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing of notice/objection is broadly aligned, but the contract adds a substantive limitation on the objection right that the regulation does not permit.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring security of processing. The contract includes a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring security, plus security measures and an assistance clause, but it does not itself impose an obligation on the processor to assist the controller with security; it mainly describes security measures and a testing/evaluation process. The assistance reference is only to assistance generally and is tied to charges, not a substantive security-assistance duty.

results.modality_check: The regulation is mandatory ('shall assist'). The contract language is descriptive and, where it mentions assistance, it is not framed as a mandatory processor obligation to assist with security.
